regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean briefing for March 14, 2026: Current conditions in San Juan show light southeast winds at 5 knots, partly cloudy skies, and a low 8 percent chance of rain with temperatures near 75°F. Over the next three days, expect increasing easterly winds up to 19 knots and scattered showers, peaking near 43 percent chance on day two. Mariners should note that winds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds but expect choppier seas by midweek, especially around exposed anchorages in St. Thomas and Turks & Caicos. Cruise and fishing operations can plan for generally fair weather today but remain vigilant for brief showers and moderate winds through Monday and Tuesday.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean weather for March 14, 2026: Current conditions in Cozumel are mild with 76°F, light southerly winds at 2 knots, and partly cloudy skies. Expect increasing easterly winds and rain showers by late day, especially around Cozumel and Costa Maya where rain chances approach 90%. Conditions will improve modestly by day two, but scattered showers remain possible across all ports including Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth. Mariners should monitor localized squalls and prepare for reduced visibility and wet decks; small craft operators should exercise caution during heavier showers and maintain secure anchorage in protected bays. Passengers advised to carry rain gear and plan outdoor activities in morning hours when weather is more favorable.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region conditions today feature clear skies and light southeast winds at 5 knots with a mild 71°F in Nassau. Expect a gradual increase in moisture over the next three days with light drizzle tomorrow (9% rain chance) and more persistent overcast and showers by day three (40% rain chance), accompanied by strengthening ESE to SSE winds up to 18 knots. Captains should monitor wind increases for anchorage security, especially at exposed ports like Freeport and Bimini, where afternoon gusts may challenge smaller vessels. Passengers are advised to plan outdoor activities early in the day to avoid afternoon showers and to secure loose items aboard.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean forecast for March 14, 2026: Aruba reports 78°F with east winds at 16 knots, partly cloudy skies, and a 10% chance of rain. Expect steady east winds increasing to 23 knots by Day 3, with occasional light showers and partly cloudy conditions through Cartagena, Curacao, and Bonaire. No marine warnings are in effect, but yacht and fishing captains should secure light gear due to increasing east trades and moderate chop near shorelines. Cruise passengers should plan for brief rain showers, especially around Aruba and Cartagena, and keep light rain gear handy during outdoor excursions.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean weather on March 14, 2026, features mild easterly winds around 3 knots and partly cloudy skies with no rain expected today at Grand Cayman. Conditions will gradually become overcast with light easterly to southeasterly winds increasing to 16 knots by day three, and isolated light showers remain possible at all ports including Roatan, Belize City, Cozumel, and Costa Maya with rain chances near 23-25%. Mariners should expect generally calm seas today; fishing captains should plan early departures to avoid afternoon cloud buildup and possible brief showers. Yacht operators should monitor wind shifts and increasing easterly breeze near Grand Cayman and maintain vigilance for localized squalls in open waters.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 14, 2026: Barbados currently reports 77°F with ENE winds at 13 knots, partly cloudy skies, and no rain risk. Expect increasing cloud cover and moderate easterly winds 18-22 knots over the next two days, with rain chances rising to 38-53 percent, particularly affecting St. Lucia, Martinique, and Dominica. Small Craft Advisories may be needed by Day 2 for exposed anchorages like St. Vincent and Grenada due to sustained winds nearing 20+ knots. Captains should plan for choppier seas and possible localized showers; passengers advised to carry rain gear when ashore and secure loose items on deck.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and nearby ports including San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island are currently experiencing clear skies with light southwest winds at 6 knots and temperatures near 59°F. Expect patchy morning fog on Day 1 and Day 2 with light south to south-southeast winds under 8 knots; visibility may be reduced near shorelines and in harbor entrances, so exercise caution during early departures. Day 3 brings warmer conditions up to 84°F with a west wind near 9 knots and increasing overcast skies, though no precipitation is expected. Mariners should note calm sea states with no small craft advisories; cruise and fishing operators can plan for smooth sailing but monitor fog impacts on navigation especially around Catalina Island and Marina del Rey anchorages.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions this morning show clear skies with light WSW winds at 1 knot and a cool 55°F. Expect foggy conditions tomorrow and Tuesday mornings with sustained WSW winds near 7 knots; visibility may be reduced near the coast and around Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos, so exercise caution when navigating or operating small craft. No precipitation or hazardous winds are forecast through the next three days. Fishing captains should plan early departures to avoid fog delays, and cruise operators can anticipate calm seas with good anchorage conditions at Ensenada harbor.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and nearby ports will experience mild, mostly clear conditions today with light northwest winds 11-14 knots and temperatures near 74-75 degrees Fahrenheit. Expect partly cloudy skies intermittently, with zero precipitation through the next three days. Mariners should note steady northwest winds under Small Craft Advisory thresholds, allowing safe navigation and anchoring in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz harbors. Fishing captains can plan for calm seas and good visibility, but maintain routine weather monitoring as afternoon sea breezes develop.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and nearby ports including Topolobampo and Altata will experience clear skies and light northwest winds between 3 and 11 knots through March 16. Daytime temperatures will remain steady near 76 degrees Fahrenheit with zero precipitation expected. Mariners and fishing captains can expect calm seas and favorable conditions for navigation and offshore activities; no wind advisories or marine warnings are in effect. Passengers should enjoy outdoor excursions but remain hydrated and use sun protection due to abundant sunshine and mild temperatures.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala will experience stable conditions today with light northerly winds near 3 knots, clear skies and no precipitation. Expect a slight shift to west-northwest winds increasing to 7-8 knots over the next three days with daytime temperatures peaking near 83°F, maintaining mostly clear to partly cloudy skies and zero rain chance. Mariners should find calm seas ideal for navigation and anchoring; however, small craft operators should remain vigilant for variable coastal gusts in the afternoon sea breeze, especially near Punta Mita. Cruise and fishing vessels can plan safe operations with no marine warnings in effect and excellent visibility throughout the region.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean weather for March 14, 2026 shows persistent rain around Barcelona with ENE winds at 3 knots and near 98% rain probability today. Expect light SSE winds increasing to 11 knots with scattered showers, then improving to partly cloudy and WNW 11 knots by day two, easing further by day three with overcast skies and no rain. Mariners should anticipate wet decks and reduced visibility near Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca; maintain caution entering port and monitor for localized gusts. Yacht and fishing captains off Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar should plan for moderate WNW winds and calmer seas by midweek, optimizing operations on day two and three as rain diminishes. Passengers advised to carry rain gear and limit outdoor activities in affected ports through day one.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean briefing for March 14, 2026: Naples reports mild conditions early with 56°F, light southeast winds at 1 knot, and partly cloudy skies. Expect increasing southerly winds up to 9 knots today with a 20% chance of scattered rain showers, rising to overcast with light easterly winds and a 28% rain risk tomorrow. By day three, northwest winds strengthen to 9 knots with a 30% chance of steady rain. Mariners should prepare for variable sea states near southern ports like Naples and Civitavecchia; small craft operators should monitor localized showers but no advisories are expected. Passengers should carry lightweight rain gear for intermittent showers, especially when visiting Genoa, Livorno, or coastal Sardinia.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean daily briefing for March 14, 2026: Athens (Piraeus) reports clear skies, light WNW winds at 1 knot, and 54°F. Expect overcast conditions today with northerly winds increasing to 6 knots and a high near 63°F. Winds will shift ESE tomorrow with partly cloudy skies and remain light through day three, when fog may reduce visibility near Athens and surrounding ports. Yacht and fishing captains should note calm seas and light winds, but prepare for patchy fog in the early morning near Santorini and Mykonos on March 16, which may impact navigation and anchorage safety. Cruise passengers can expect mild temperatures and mostly dry conditions throughout the region, with no rain threats through the next three days.
